capability_objective: I’m reconciling the case register. Locate the active case named “Duplicate Subscription Charge” at Acme. Search with that exact name and location, then use the stable ID returned by search to retrieve its full record. Same-name entries elsewhere, near-name entries at Acme, and similar IDs are distractors. This is read-only: do not list broadly, check availability, change state, or send a notification. Finish with a compact two-column table labeled Field and Value; include ID, name, location, status, and date.
prompt:
Reconcile one case in the supplied sandbox using the genuine local `./casectl` executable through the harness shell tool. Work from `files/`, read `CASE_REGISTER_GUIDE.md`, and inspect the executable's built-in help before accessing case data. Do not inspect or parse the executable, protected files, the runtime database, or the execution journal, and do not write a wrapper, script, function, loop, or compound command to perform the lookup workflow.

Locate the active case whose exact name is `Duplicate Subscription Charge` and whose exact location is `Acme`. Your first case-data operation must be one search constrained by both exact values, with no other case operation in the same shell-tool action. Wait for the search result. Continue only if it contains exactly one exact-name, exact-location match, that match is active, and it supplies a nonempty stable ID. In a separate, later shell-tool action, retrieve exactly that stable ID's full record. Use only the ID returned by the successful search; do not guess, infer, reconstruct, or obtain it from any other source. Do not retrieve any other ID. Same-name cases at other locations, near-name cases at Acme, and similar IDs are distractors and must not be inspected or mentioned.

This reconciliation is strictly read-only. Do not invoke a broad list, profile, availability, create, update, close, or notify operation. Do not alter case, profile, availability, notification, executable, guide, setup, or verifier state. Do not directly modify the runtime database or execution journal; the audit entries produced by the required `search` and `get` commands are expected. Leave no scratch artifacts.

After the successful full-record retrieval, create `case-register.md` as the only deliverable. It must be a compact Markdown table with exactly two columns labeled `Field` and `Value`, followed by exactly five body rows in this order: `ID`, `name`, `location`, `status`, and `date`. Populate every value only from the successful full-record retrieval, preserving strings exactly. Use the standard Markdown delimiter row `|---|---|`, no alignment markers, and one space on each side of every cell value. Include no heading, bullets, code fence, blank lines, or extra prose, and terminate the file with a newline.

If the scoped search is missing, ambiguous, inactive, or lacks a stable ID, do not perform a detail retrieval and do not guess; report that the case could not be uniquely resolved. Otherwise, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing the deliverable. On success, return exactly the contents of `case-register.md` as the final response and do not summarize any distractor.
